##### II.2.a.b — AI does not expect: the mistake

::: {.aside .meta-note}
**META:** The branch Posts 1–2 surfaced that the wider debate tends to skip: the system understands catastrophe in the abstract, but its own chosen action doesn't flag as one.
:::

Here the system knows what catastrophe is, but its forward-model of its own chosen action fails to flag the danger. It knows what doom is; it just doesn't see that this is it. Post 2's cases for this are concrete: an action safe in isolation that triggers a butterfly effect past the model's horizon, or one safe in training that turns fatal out of distribution.

**Control Failure.** The risk is high, but the failure model is less brittle. In Anthropic's January 2024 [*Sleeper Agents*](https://arxiv.org/abs/2401.05566) paper, models trained with a hidden trigger stayed deceptive through standard safety training, and adversarial training mainly taught them to hide it better. The threat is not a sharp left turn but the messy, likely reality that institutions cannot reliably align advanced capability before deploying it. [Paul Christiano](https://www.lesswrong.com/posts/xWMqsvHapP3nwdSW8/my-views-on-doom) (who puts irreversible takeover near 22 percent) and [Dario Amodei](https://www.axios.com/2025/09/17/anthropic-dario-amodei-p-doom-25-percent) (around 25 percent that things go "really, really badly") hold this. Unlike the prior-dominant structuralists, their model is empirically conditioned and path-dependent, updating on capability trajectories, warning shots, and how short-term alignment work fares. What moves it: interpretability or scalable alignment lowers their estimates; a failure to remove in-context scheming, or a deployment-stage incident, raises them.

::: {.aside}
The misuse route has no clean home here. Control Failure also carries a second pathway, the deliberate misuse of an aligned-but-compliant system by a bad actor, a state synthesizing a novel pathogen. That route doesn't fit the tree: the system has no misaligned internal model, and the agent who "expects" the catastrophe is the human, not the AI. It is a real worry the mechanism-based tree doesn't cleanly capture, because its decisive variable is human intent.
:::
